entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop used for seed longevity studies

entities.name: PENNISETUM GLAUCUM
entities.description: The scientific name for pearl millet

entities.name: SEED LONGEVITY
entities.description: The ability of seeds to remain viable during storage

entities.name: P50
entities.description: The time required for seed viability to decline to 50% during storage

entities.name: CONTROLLED POLLINATION
entities.description: A method used to ensure pollination before harvesting seeds at specific intervals

entities.name: DAYS AFTER POLLINATION
entities.description: The time interval between pollination and seed harvest

entities.name: STORAGE TEMPERATURE
entities.description: The temperature at which seeds are kept during storage, specifically 35°C or 40°C

entities.name: MOISTURE CONTENT
entities.description: The percentage of water in seeds during storage, specifically 13% or 15%

entities.name: PHYSIOLOGICAL MATURITY
entities.description: The stage marking the end of the grain filling period

entities.name: GRAIN FILLING PERIOD
entities.description: The phase of seed development where nutrients are accumulated

entities.name: DSA 105A
entities.description: A cytoplasmic male sterile line of pearl millet

entities.name: 5141A
entities.description: A cytoplasmic male sterile line of pearl millet

entities.name: L 67A
entities.description: A cytoplasmic male sterile line of pearl millet

entities.name: CYTOPLASMIC MALE STERILITY
entities.description: A condition where the plant is unable to produce functional pollen
